How to Create
Irresistible Sales
Messages for ANY
Business
Module 4: The Power of Story
in Sales Copy
With Dan Kennedy and John Carlton
www.gkic.com
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 4
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
The Power of Story in Sales Copy
We return to copywriting and I want to tell you about donuts.
Some new folks often come up and ask me, because nothing dies on the web
anymore, as you know… so all the Dan “fat” pictures are still on the web and will
be there forever more.
Some people are surprised that I’m not fatter and younger and have more hair
than I do.
For many years, I carried 245 to 255 pounds, give or take. Most of the time I was
on the successive end, so I was about 245.
Donuts are one of the reasons why. My jacket size was 52, to give you a frame of
reference. There’s a few things in life that I find irresistible, one of which are
donuts.
When we lived in Phoenix, for a time on the street between my house and my
office, when I used to go to the office, there was a donut shop, Dunkin’ Donuts.
It was conveniently located on the right side of the street. You didn’t have to
cross traffic to get to it – not that that
would have stopped me, but it just
made it easier.
Pretty much every morning I stopped,
just to be a nice guy, and get some
donuts for the office – most of which
went into my office, thereby helping maintain a 245 to 250 pound weight.
The solution to that was I sold the house and we moved, so as not to pass the
donut shop on the way to the office. If anybody in here in their normal life, in
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 5
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
their day job, is in real estate, there is all sorts of reasons to list and sell a house.
There is one of them – it’s on a route where there is a donut shop.
I have the iron will not to go get any as long as I’m not passing any, but I do not
have the iron will not to eat any, and I am aware of that about myself.
I occasionally now get salad at Panera Bread, and because they call themselves
Panera Bread, not Panera Salad, they feel compelled to put a small loaf of bread
in with every salad. If you buy two salads to take home, you get two little loaves
of bread.
Because I understand myself very well, in the garage before I come into the
house, I take the bread out of the bag, rip it into parts and I put it in the garbage,
deep in the garbage so that I do not go back out there and get it out of the
garbage – of which I am capable, by the way.
Years ago there was a speaker. None of you will know him. His name is Charlie
Jarvis. Charlie talked about being on a diet, calling home and confessing to his
wife that hanging out in the airport for three hours waiting for a flight, he finally
couldn’t take it anymore. He ate a Snickers bar. She of course jumped all over
him.
He said, “You don’t understand. This is a big victory because I wanted to eat all
the Snickers bars.”
Using Story in Copywriting
Let’s take that story and let’s talk about that story for a second because we’re
going to talk about the use and the power of story in sales copywriting.
That story could be used a number of different ways. It could be used as a bridge
to talk about failure environment versus success environment, and why you
need to create and be in a success environment.
Then we could bridge from there to selling just about anything that had to do
with being in a success environment.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 6
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
It could be used as a bridge to the discussion of the futility of will power, and
then if we were selling some sort of self-image reprogramming stuff, if we were
selling subliminal tapes, if we were selling hypnagogic tapes, it would get us
there.
It could be used as a bridge to the importance of
self-awareness, and if we were selling some sort
of diagnostic process that made us more self-
aware of our weaknesses and our strengths, we
could use the story in order to bridge our way
into that discussion.
It could be used as a bridge to a discussion of
what we allow inside our house, thereby
bridging us to the principle of association.
By the way, I win the war, most people win the war – just as an aside – at the
perimeter of the house, not inside the house. Fixing whatever has entered the
house after it’s in there is much more difficult than having a good perimeter.
Basically, what that story does, is it gives us a bridge to any number of things
that we might want to sell, which is one of the prime purposes of a story and, in
this case, it creates rapport through shared experience.
Just about everybody is weak to something so it gives us a shared experience
that builds rapport.
The important thing to know about all of this is that stories are
more powerful than statements.
Stories v Statements
When you start to write copy to sell, it’s important to think about whether, at
this particular place to make this particular point, we ought to be making an
assertion, making a statement, or we ought to be telling a story.
It’s important to
think about whether
to make an
assertion, make a
statement or tell a
story.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 7
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
Here’s some quick things about how that works, and then we’ll talk about the
kinds of stories you might want to use as an info-marketer.
Skepticism v Curiosity
First of all statement immediately manifests skepticism. People’s guard goes up
and they essentially take the mental position of, “I don’t believe it. Prove it.”
If we start with a story, curiosity is aroused, interest can occur
and skepticism is set aside.
Something that you would be prone to believe, but still, if I said to you that it
doesn’t matter who gets elected, what the makeup of the congress is, none of
that matters; for as long as you live, no matter what, you will never, ever, ever,
ever see government spending reduced, reigned in, controlled or managed.
You might even be prone to believe that but still, “never” is a big word, isn’t it?
I mean never? Really? Is it that futile? People will question that statement.
So let me tell you a story. Colin Powell was on the Success events with us for a
number of years. I followed Colin, so we spent a lot of time in the green room
together.
One time Colin comes in and he’s brought me a big… I don’t know, about four
times the size of a shoe box in circumference, and about the depth of a shoe box.
He said, “Here I brought you some stuff.” I said, “Great, what stuff did you bring
me?”
It’s all the stuff that they give every cabinet officer, every head of a department,
every congressman; everybody gets stuff to give away to visitors and
constituents.
There’s cuff links, there’s tie tacks, there’s paper weights, there’s pins, there’s
baseball caps. There’s all kinds of stuff – clocks, wrist watches, you name it.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 8
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
For each one of these people, it has their seal of their office on it, so in his case
somewhere there’s a little secretary of state seal. Their autograph is engraved
on it somewhere.
So there’s cuff links and paper weights and pen sets, all with his signature and
the little seal. I said, “Cool, but why and why six of each thing? Why not one?”
He said, “Well, would you like to hear the story?” I said, “Of course I’d like to
hear the story.”
He said, “The first week I’m in office, I’m in my office, I’m literally still putting
stuff away, door opens, guy comes in and he’s wheeling one of these big flat bed
things like they push stuff around on at Costco, and it’s full to the brim with
boxes.”
He said, “What’s that?” The guy says, “This is your monthly supply, sir, of stuff
you give away. Here’s your pens. Here’s your paperweights, here’s your
cufflinks, here’s your tie tacks, here’s your wrist watches, here’s your baseball
caps, etc.”
He said, “Where am I going…?” “That’s what that closet over there in the corner
of your office is for. I stock it with this stuff for you. I’ll wheel it over there and
I’ll put it in there.”
Next month, first week of the month, guy comes in the door, pushing the flat bed
with boxes on it piled as high as you can pile. Colin says, “What’s that?” He said,
“That’s your monthly supply of the stuff you give away.”
He said, “Wait a minute. The closet is full. If I stood by the window and did
nothing but throw it out as my job, I couldn’t get rid of it. Don’t bring me any
more until I…”
He said, “No, no, no, sir, you don’t understand. On the first of every month I
arrive with this push cart full of this stuff. Your job during the month is to give
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 9
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
away everything that’s in the closet so there’s room to put this stuff in when I
arrive with it.”
“I don’t want it.” “You don’t understand sir. On the first of every month... Now I
can assign a storage locker for you sir and I can take it down there and I can put
it in storage after you sign for it. But every month, I arrive with my cart full of
stuff, so you’ve got to give it away.”
Colin says, “I am the secretary of state. I am now aggravated. I’ve worked my
way up the ladder of procurement in the Pentagon,” because his office is under
the Pentagon.
Understand everybody’s under something different, but they’re all getting loads
of stuff. He works his way all the way up to the director of procurement. He says,
“Look, I don’t want this stuff. I can’t give it away.”
The guy says, “You don’t understand. Every month that guy is coming. They can
put it in a storage locker.” He said, “Now I’m really aggravated. I make a project
now out of finding out how much it costs.” He said, “Here, pen set. How much do
you think this costs?”
They look like Cross pen and pencil. They’re not Cross but they look like a Cross
pen and a Cross pencil, and they’ve got the little secretary of state seal on it.
They’ve got his name autographed on the side, nice box with a little velvet thing
in the back. Across the top of the box, his name is on it in gold.
I said, “I don’t know, if you buy it at Office Max at retail without your thing on it,
I don’t know $30, $40 tops.” He said, “We’re paying $598 for every one of these
and I get 144 of them every month.”
He said here’s the best part of the story. He said, “A few months later I’m still
stewing over this and we’re in President Bush’s office, me, Cheney, Rumsfeld.
There’s a bunch of us in there for a meeting. The meeting ends early.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 10
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
Everybody is kind of in a good mood, which is rare, and Bush says to everybody,
‘Hey, we got some time. Anybody got anything they want to talk about?’”
Colin says, “Yeah. Every month they bring me this thing. I’ve been everywhere I
can to try and put a stop to it and they won’t stop sending it. I can’t stop it.”
President Bush gets up, he walks across the oval office, he opens a big four-door
closet and he said, “I can’t stop it either.”
Which is more persuasive to the point that we will never, ever, ever, ever reign
in government spending, the assertion or the story?
The story is irrefutable. If the President of the United States can’t stop the pens
from coming, it truly is beyond all hope.
 Statement: skepticism.
 Story: curiosity, interest.
The mind can only hold one emotionally triggered response at a time. If it is
being curious and it is being interested, it can’t be skeptical at the same time. It
doesn’t multitask. Very cool thing to know.
If you were to go back and look again at some of the stuff I showed you in
module #1, one item was from the financial newsletter industry, “Caught on
Videotape.”
What does the sales letter start
with? It starts with a story.
“Dear Subscriber, I don’t
consider myself a reporter,
but recently I flew up the
East coast to meet with a
gentleman who has
generated so much money, I
arranged for a full camera crew to record us.”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 11
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
The entire first half of this page is a story. Why? Because it arouses curiosity,
arouses interest, and sets aside skepticism.
Challenging Statements
A statement can be challenged or differed with instantly.
As soon as I make a statement, you can start to differ with it. You can start to
disagree with it.
If we are in disagreement, that’s in the way to getting to agreement, which is
what I need if I am going to sell you
anything.
If I start a story, we have been conditioned
since childhood to pay attention to stories,
to want to hear stories, “Please, read me a
story.”
We now have an app for that. There’s a grandpa app. You just press it, hand the
kid the pad, send them off in a corner and it does it for you, I get it. Still, we have
been conditioned since childhood. So statements are challenged. Stories are
listened to.
Confrontational v Inspirational
Statement can be confrontational. A story can be inspirational.
If I say to you, “You are fat,” that’s a confrontational statement. Most people do
not like hearing it. It is not a good way to sell weight loss products. It has been
tried, by the way, by many, and even with humor it doesn’t work.
Halbert did a famous radio campaign – that lasted a day – of fat jokes for a
weight loss clinic. Not a good idea. You do get phone calls, but you don’t get
phone calls to book appointments.
STATEMENT can be challenged or
differed with (instantly)
STORY must be listened to (We have
been conditioned since childhood)
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 12
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
The statement that you are fat, or you are
fat and ugly, is not a welcome thing, but an
inspiration story…
Right now, as I left, Cleveland’s promo – because we have sweeps weeks right
now for the local news; the local news anchor woman has been beefing up over
the last couple of years and it’s gotten to the point of like Oprah obviousness.
The co-anchor, you can only see half of them if you’ve got a small TV. So it’s
“tune in to see how she has lost 40 pounds and changed her life.” Over the next
four nights, this will be a ratings hit because it’s an inspirational story.
If it was, “All you fat folk tune in and we’re going to tell you how to get the fat
off,” they would not tune in.
Authority
This is kind of cool. A statement requires authority. A story can even create
authority.
If I tell you I have a miracle cure for cancer, you immediately start to think
about, “Who is this guy and why should I listen to him about cancer? Is he an
M.D.? Is he at Mayo Clinic? Is he this? Is he that?”
But if I tell you a story about having been
diagnosed with cancer and being given
three months to live, and gone on a
worldwide search for alternative healers,
and found a guy in a cave at the top of the mountain in Nepal, who gave me the
magic cancer-curing formula and I went on his diet and to the shock and
surprise of my doctors, when I went in three months later, all my cancer was
gone….
Your questions about am I an M.D., do I practice at Mayo, most of those
questions are forgotten because now you want to hear about the guy in Nepal
and what the diet is.
STATEMENT requires Authority
STORY can create Authority
STATEMENT can be confrontational
STORY can be inspirational
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 13
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
I can probably sell you the grapefruit, chocolate and bourbon cancer-curing diet
right behind it. It won’t cure cancer, but you’ll feel better between now and the
time you die!
By the way, it’s risky to do cancer jokes!
Copying Stories
Statements can be copied and diluted. Personal story really can’t be. Personal
story is proprietary and protected.
It’s very hard these days to protect intellectual property. It’s one of the adverse
trends in this industry, is our fight to protect our content, and to protect our
sales content.
It’s harder and harder and harder and harder.
The speed of knockoff is faster and faster and faster and faster, because the
barrier to knockoff is lower, and lower, and lower, and lower.
There is a new information marketing infomercial, just started airing I think this
month or last month. I think it’s called Brainiac or something like that. It’s how
to turn your kid into a whiz kid. It’s sort of a re-invention of the Hooked on
Phonics plus memory thing.
What would have been a $399 product is
now a $14.95 product. Then the upsells
have to begin and the use of the list and all
that.
Anyway, it’s make your kid smarter, get better grades, everybody will be able to
memorize stuff. They do the magic math demonstration.
None of this is new. It may be new to you because maybe you never saw it
before. It’s not new but it’s very well done.
STATEMENT can be copied and
diluted
(Personal) STORY can be proprietary
and protected
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 14
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
It’s a very effective show, but there is absolutely nothing in this that can’t be
knocked off at high speed, unless somebody had a great personal story.
If the person behind the product had ADD and 14 other mental diseases and
dyslexia, had been thrown out of 20 schools and was failing every class, and
found the magic formula, now you would have something.
So stories actually have equity. If you think about it, there are at least three you
know, or at least should know, that have great equity.
Napoleon Hill’s Andrew Carnegie story has great equity. If you take the Andrew
Carnegie story out of “Think and Grow Rich” – if you take that story away from
Napoleon Hill, what exactly do you have?
Not much. You’ve just got another success book
written by a guy who went and interviewed
some successful people and wrote a book about
it.
There has been thousands of those. They’re all in
a graveyard. Most of them lasted three to four
months in the marketplace – some of them
written by famous people.
Think and Grow Rich has endured since 1937. It
still sells hundreds of thousands of copies every year. Why? Because of the
Andrew Carnegie story that people know and tell other people when they
recommend the book.
The Maxwell Maltz story for “Psycho-Cybernetics” – the only self-help book we
know of to match “50 Shades of Grey” in numbers of copies sold.
The story of the woman who had surgery and couldn’t see that her face had
been improved, thereby the discovery of self-image – that story has equity. It
was Max’s and Max’s alone.
If you take the
Andrew Carnegie
story out of “Think
and Grow Rich,”
you’ve got just
another success
book.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 15
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
He was the plastic surgeon. She was his patient. No one can take that story. It’s
his and his alone. It’s proprietary. It’s unique and exclusive.
Robert Kiyosaki, a more contemporary example. Bob has made a living off of,
and a fortune from, a single relatively simple personal story. I like Bob, so no
disrespect, but really there’s not a lot there but the story.
What’s the story? Two dads – Rich Dad, Poor Dad, observe the difference. That
story has equity because it’s his. You can’t take it.
Story telling has real power in selling, and it’s an
important part of copywriting.
Types of Story
We’ll talk now about all the stories that you need. One type of story that’s very
important, we referenced it a little bit in module #1, is the “reason why” story or
the origin story.
People want to know where you came from, how it is that you got to the point
that you are standing in front of them, advising them, coercing them, presenting
to them. How did you get there? Who the heck are you?
Not the business card “who are you?” Not really the resume “who are you?”
Who are you in the sense of being a human that I might identify with, feel
empathy with, feel affinity for; somehow want to be associated with, learn from,
hang out with. Who are you? Why are you here?
We all know intellectually why everybody is here in front of us trying to sell us
something.
They are here for the money. Intellectually, pretty much, you’ve got to have an
IQ of a turnip not to know that whoever it is that has appeared in front of you,
however they have done it, to sell you something, is there for the money.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 16
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
Intellectually, we know, hardly anybody would be there if you took away the
money. Go make 50 speeches this year and you don’t get paid. Go knock on
doors and do vacuum cleaner demonstrations, but you get no commission.
So intellectually we know. That does not stop us from wanting to believe that
there is another motive for the person being in front of us trying to sell us
something that is more honorable.
They want to believe there is a reason you are there,
other than money.
Therefore, it’s important to give them that reason. It’s helpful if it’s legit – if you
do have a second authentic reason for doing what it is that you’re doing. But
whether it’s legit or not, I will tell you it is important for you to have it.
Last, they want to know, “How can I feel it’s okay to trust this person?” As you
know, there’s a book – most of you now have it – about trust-based marketing.
We grossly underestimate how scary we are to other people.
You know you stand in front of the mirror, you’re ready to go downstairs; it ain’t
Brad Pitt looking back at me, but I don’t expect little children to run, screaming
into the bushes either. I don’t feel I’m really scary.
One of the things I do at these events, when you don’t see me, I’m not sleeping;
I’m having private appointments with Titanium members, Platinum members,
clients, etc.
Yesterday at an appointment, I won’t
identify her, a woman business person
who had a private appointment with me,
and it’s the first time we’ve had a private
appointment, immediately at the start of
the appointment said, “I’m a little
Reason Why Stories
People Want to Know:
Where did you come from?
Who are you?
Why are you here?
How can I feel it’s okay to trust you?
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 17
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
uncomfortable being in a hotel room with you alone, because the last time I did
that with a speaker, he attempted to freshen my breath, but not with an Altoid.”
She told me that why? Because she was genuinely anxious about the
circumstance, and that was her way of issuing a warning, should I be so inclined.
Now, I don’t think of myself in that context at all. I was a little surprised she
thought of me in that context.
But think about this for a second. We already have a relationship. It’s not like
this is off the street. It’s not like we’re strangers in a sense. This person is a
member. She comes to functions. She reads me every month. Still, that’s there.
We grossly underestimate how scary we are to other people. So the last thing
they want to know is, “How can I feel it’s okay to trust this person?”
Origin Stories
The origin story is pretty important. Generally speaking, people don’t make
sense without their origin stories. The Lone Ranger makes no sense. Batman
makes no sense. The new Green Arrow on the CW makes no sense without the
origin story.
The typical info-marketing origin stories, the most common are these, “I found
the key and I feel guilty keeping it to myself,” which also provides the extra
reason why.
“I had to figure it out for myself, the really
hard way, and I’m still mad at all the
people who misled me, all the people who
sent me down a blind alley and the best
revenge is now sharing what I’ve figured
out with you.”
Typical Infomarketer Origin Stories
Found the key and feel guilty keeping
it to myself
Had to figure it out for myself and
still mad at all the charlatans
This thing found me
My friend(s) in the field begged me…
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 18
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
The third kind of story is this whole thing – teaching, training, writing – it sort of
found me because people kept asking as they found out how successful I was at
emu training.
“All the other emu trainers like you kept asking me, so I finally felt compelled to
develop a sensible way to answer the question.”
For the outsider coming into a niche, “My friend(s) in the field, knowing that I
was the marketing genius of the free world, begged and begged and begged me
to help them.
So I took a look at plumbers and I realized plumbers didn’t know anything about
marketing. I realized I could change the industry and that’s why I’m here.”
Those are your most common info-marketer origin stories. They work, by the
way; they’re fine, said a little more elegantly than I just said them.
Keep this in Mind
Keep the following in mind about that type of story. People are vaguely,
unconsciously uncomfortable at bare minimum when a good origin/reason why
story is missing.
Even if they can’t annunciate it; even if it doesn’t directly and consciously bother
them, it unconsciously bothers them. There’s something wrong. They don’t
really know what’s wrong.
If you think of it in the Batman context, if we present Batman to people without
an origin story, we have presented something very, very different than if we
present him with the origin story.
Every time that franchise is reinvented in
the movies, which has been five times in
my lifetime, the origin story must be told.
Every time in the graphic novels a new
series is created, the origin story must be
Keep This in Mind
People are vaguely, unconsciously
uncomfortable when a good Origin
Story is missing
People are profoundly interested in
the Origin Story and in Reason-Why
We are often guilty of not telling the
Origin Story
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 19
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
told. Otherwise he’s just a big dumb, scary, frightening guy in a goofy suit,
running around and beating the crap out of everybody. This is not a good thing.
People are profoundly interested in the origin story. They are fascinated with
why you are, what you are, where you are.
It should incidentally make no difference. I should be able to tell you there’s
absolutely no reason whatsoever for me being here in front of you but I want as
much of your money as I can possibly get my damn hands on.
That should make no difference. What I deliver to you is either valuable or not
valuable. It either makes you money or it doesn’t make you money. My motives
should not matter, intellectually. Some of you would be able to get past it
intellectually but not internally.
We are often guilty of not telling the origin story, thinking everybody knows it,
or we’re bored with it. I too am guilty, so it’s something to be careful of.
Other Types of Story
Here are some other kinds of stories that you will need as an infomarketer.
You need good stories about your champions and their journeys. A lot of people
shortcut their testimonials and their champion stories by telling the end. The
‘after’ is no good without the ‘before.’
It is the drama between the two that really
matters. It is the journey that really
matters.
How bad was it? How good is it? What was
the journey?
We’ve got to be good at telling those
stories.
Some Stories Needed by
Infomarketers
About Your Champions and Their
Journeys
About Your Miracles
About Your Adventures
That Reveal Character and Strength
That Reveal Humanity
That Arouse Curiosity and Create
Mystique
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 19 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 20
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
About your own miracles: You’ve got to be good at describing the challenges you
met, or writing that so that it is told in someone else’s voice.
You’ve got to be able to tell about your own adventures because one of the
things that is true about this business is customers who hang around for a long
period of time, what we call lifers, are there less and less and less for what Gary
Halbert called the clicks on the dial.
The clicks on the dial are the things that we have to teach you; the things that
you have to teach whoever it is that you teach. I’ve only got so many clicks on
the dial. We just spin the dial where it stops and tell you that again. That’s okay
for a year, two years, three years, but 10 years?
Everybody knows the clicks on my dial if they’ve been around me for 10 years.
They’re not hanging around for that anymore. They’re hanging around for all
sorts of other reasons.
That’s why info-marketing really is a convenient way to describe what we do.
It’s not a particularly accurate way to describe what we do. The business is
more complex than that.
One of the things people do is they live vicariously through your adventures,
through the places you go, the things you do, the people you meet.
Why is a picture of you with Adam West and the Batmobile so valuable? It’s
valuable because you get to turn around and use it with your herd, who didn’t
have that experience, but thinks that experience is cool. That’s why, but you’ve
got to create a story about that.
We had Gene Simmons from KISS at the SuperConference. I don’t know if he did
it because he was smart, or he did it just to be funny, but the smartest guy of
everybody who came to get their picture taken with Gene, is the guy who
brought the shoe box of Kiss eight-track tapes to be autographed.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 20 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 21
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
I think that’s funny, but it gave him a story. Everybody else just got a picture.
He’s got a picture and a story.
You want to be able to tell stories about your adventures. By the way, you may
not think your adventures are much. Compare yours to the way most people live
their lives. Your adventures are significant.
You want to be able to tell stories about you that reveal character and strength.
You want to be able to tell stories about you that reveal humanity so people can
believe, “If he can, I can. If she can, I can.” It’s an important part of this business.
We also grossly underestimate how little self-confidence people have, even
people who have eight years of college; even people who have professional
practices; even people who have some measure
of success.
We grossly underestimate how little self-
confidence they have about doing anything new
and different, and therefore they have to borrow
ours.
You have to be good at telling stories that arouse curiosity and create mystique.
How to Decide Which Stories to Use
So how do you decide which stories you need and which to use?
One answer is objections that need erased. What are all of the reasons why
somebody might not accept your proposition, might not respond as you wish,
might not buy? We need a good story for every one of those objections.
Assumptions or negative opinions about you or your industry that need
corrected. We need a good story for that.
One of the things
people do is they live
vicariously through
your adventures.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 22
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
Right now, in the financial category that I
showed you in module #1 – and now in the
direct-selling network marketing category
– everybody is using Warren Buffet.
It’s very hard to find a, “Here’s an
investment strategy pitch,” without
somebody somehow tying it to it being the
strategy Warren Buffet is really using.
Why? Because everybody knows Buffet.
The network marketing industry loves to tell everybody that Warren Buffet /
Berkshire Hathaway, now owns Pampered Chef. Why did they buy Pampered
Chef? For the same reasons that you should join XYZ drink company.
The connection is pretty… but it’s enough, if you tell that story well.
Assertions that need proven, particularly those that can’t be proven
scientifically, clinically or factually. Then we need stories that prove them to the
person.
And a philosophical position that can best be understood via parable.
Elements of a Good Story
Here are some elements that make for a great story. One is the power of a punch
line or a twist at the end.
I will read you a short parable from the book “The Greatness Principle” from one
of my Titanium members, Nelson Searcy, who is a pastor.
You may have heard the old story about the college senior….
Now that’s an interesting way to start the story, because it means he stole it!
How to Decide Which Stories You
Need and Which to Use
Objections That Need Erased
Assumptions or Negative Opinions
About You, Your Industry etc. That
Need Corrected
Assertions That Need Proven
A Philosophical Position That Can
Best Be Understood Via Parable
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 23
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
You may have heard the old story about
the college senior, let’s call him Justin, who
had his eye on a bright red Corvette at a
dealership close to his parents’ house.
In the weeks leading up to his graduation,
Justin drops subtle and not-so-subtle hints
to his dad that he wanted the Corvette as
his graduation present. After all, his
parents were wealthy and there was no
reason they couldn’t buy it for him.
The morning of graduation, Justin woke up thinking about the Corvette. He could
hardly concentrate on the commencement ceremony. His mind was already flying
down the highway in his new car.
After the diplomas had been passed out, Justin’s parents hosted a blowout
graduation party for him at their house.
Finally the moment came when Justin’s dad tapped him on the shoulder and asked
him to join him in the den. Justin knew this was it. He was about to get exactly
what he wanted.
Once they were seated in the den, Justin’s dad handed him a package. He
unwrapped it to find a beautifully bound Bible inscribed with his name.
Disappointed and angry, Justin pointed a finger at his father and said, “With all
your money, you’ve given me a Bible? You knew I wanted that car.” Then he
stormed out of the house, leaving the book behind.
Years later, when Justin had become a successful businessman himself, he got a
call that his dad was sick. Justin’s explosion on graduation day had caused a rift in
their relationship and he hadn’t seen his father since.
Some Elements That Make For a
Great Story
The Power of a Punchline and/or a
Twist at the End
Self-Deprecation
Good Humor
Vivid Detail
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 23 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 24
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
When he got the phone call though, he decided he should go home and mend
things.
Unfortunately, before Justin could get there, he received another call saying that
his dad had passed away, and that everything had been willed to him.
When Justin finally got to his parents’ house to take care of what needed taken
care of, he was full of sadness and regret.
As he was going through some books and papers in the den, he found the still new
Bible from his graduation day.
He couldn’t hold back the tears, as he opened the Bible and started turning the
pages. Suddenly a car key dropped from an envelope that had been taped to the
Bible’s back cover.
On the back of the key ring, Justin’s father had written, “Congratulations, son.
Celebrate with a new Corvette.”
Powerful story. We can do a lot with that story.
Where he chose to take it, you and I aren’t that different from Justin.
While he may have been spoiled and overly demanding of his parents, he did
something that we are prone to do more often than we realize.
He completely missed the gift his father – remember this is religious – wanted to
give him, because it wasn’t packaged in the way he thought it should be, etc., etc.
I won’t read you the rest of it.
That’s the power of a story with a twist at the end.
That’s the power of a story that gets a gasp, a sink in the stomach, a visceral
emotional reaction at a key point that then leads you to using the parable to
demonstrate whatever it is that you built the parable to do.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 25
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
It’s important to understand that stories can be reverse
engineered from the point you want them to make.
You’ve got to know what it is you want to drive home, and then you go and
search for or create the story.
The second kind of story that works is the self-deprecating humor story. You
have to be careful not to use too many of them or
you wound your status with your audience.
You can’t use too many in succession, like in the
same sales letter. There’s usually only room for
one. In the same sales speech, there’s usually only
room for one or you start to diminish your status.
One is okay.
If Colin Powell wanted to, he could use the story that I told you. He could stop
short of Bush.
Really, the story, if you stop short of the Oval Office, is an ancient story every
speaker has used about being at the head table at a banquet and the person
comes along who is dispensing the butter and who puts one pat of butter in
front of everybody, and the speaker, who is there to be honored, asks for two
pats of butter and the guy says, “No sir, everybody only gets one pat of butter.”
The guy says, “You apparently don’t know who I am. I’m the secretary of state,”
at which the point the guy says, “And I’m the guy in charge of the butter.”
It’s the same story. Colin Powell’s story with the guy pushing the cart is the pat
of butter story, which dates back to the beginning of butter and banquets, and
he could stop it there and use it as a self-deprecating humor story if he wanted
to.
The cuff link story. This is not original. It did happen to me but it’s happened to
other dumb people too.
If you use too many
self-deprecating
stories, you wound
your status with
your audience.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 25 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 26
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
Understand I just did the same thing Nelson did, in case you’ve heard it from
somebody else, I just covered it. It did happen to me though, so I’ll tell it the way
it happened to me.
I’m speaking, I’m on the road. I always wear French cuff shirts when I speak. I’m
in some rat-hole town in Alabama in this crappy motel. I’m going to go speak the
next morning. I’m laying out my clothes. I go into my kit for my cuff links. I have
no cuff links. I have left the cuff links at home.
I have a shirt. I have no cuff links. All my stage suits are cut short to show the
cuff. It’s not like I can use a paper clip. It’s going to show.
I’m aggravated. I go downstairs and ask where the best menswear store is in
town that might still be open, that I can go get some cuff links.
The person at the front desk tells me Sears is the best menswear store in town.
So I get a cab and I go over to Sears. I go into the menswear department to get
cuff links, and of course they have no cuff links. “K-mart might have some.”
I go to K-mart. Of course I’m getting more aggravated and worked up as I go
from store to store to store, none of them having any cuff links in this rat hole
town.
By the time I get to the last place, which I forget. It was either Walmart or K-
mart… I’m not a calm person anyway so I’m one step away from letting
somebody have it.
This poor young girl is putting words together to explain to me that, “No, sir, we
don’t have cuff links.”
Of course I don’t let her finish her sentence. I now give her the 15 minutes of
what a rat hole place this is, and how everybody’s a moron, and how could there
be a city without a single freaking cuff link in it, and what the hell is wrong with
you people anyway, and how important I am, and I’m speaking the next morning
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 26 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 27
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
and I have got to have cuff links because I have French cuff shirts, and my jacket
is…, I’ve got to have cuff links and they have to be…
Finally done, she said, “Well sir, I’m sorry, we just don’t have cuff links but sir,
we have a lot of nice shirts with buttons on them.”
Hmm, that thought never occurred to me as a way to solve this problem.
That story is a self-deprecating story. The thing you use it for mostly is a bridge
as to how we get myopia.
We get narrow focused on one solution to one thing
and we can’t think creatively.
You can use it then to sell mastermind. It’s a reason why you should be in
Platinum; it’s a reason why you should be in Peak Performers.
It’s a perfect bridge to that because, left to our own devices, we get so narrow-
focused on one solution and only one solution to one problem, and that solution
can’t work for us, therefore there is no solution to the problem.
That’s why you want to be in a group of other people who stimulate others. You
see how we can use it?
So self-deprecation, good humor, and of course you want vivid detail.
Classic Story Plots
Here are some classic story plots most useful to info-marketers.
One is the Charles Atlas / Rocky / “They all laughed when I sat down at the
piano, until I started to play….”
The classic plot line of being underestimated, being thought of as the least likely
to succeed, being the guy who had sand kicked in his face by the bully who now
– through some mechanism, be it the exercise device, the money making
miracle, the whatever – rises above all of them and now has the last laugh.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 27 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 28
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
That’s a plot line that works in the info-marketing industry, again and again and
again.
It worked for Charles Atlas. It works in
selling fitness today.
“The little engine who could… which
proves you can too” story.
That’s the person who is really least likely
to succeed. The one-legged golfer is almost
in that category, but the really, really poor
person, the really, really dumb person…
The story doesn’t have to be about you,
remember. It can be one of your champions
about which you have this fabulous little
“engine that could” story.
That sells things because people need to believe they can too, and when they see
somebody who is much more disadvantaged than they are…
Then there’s a “Blackie” story. If you don’t know what a Blackie story is, I don’t
have time to give you the whole history here – I’m sure you can Google it and
find it.
The Andrew Carnegie and Napoleon Hill story is a Blackie story; the Kenny
Rogers song “The Gambler,” with the guy on the train, that’s a Blackie story.
It’s a story where there is some old grizzled possessor of a secret, some dying
wizard, who with his last breath passes along…, someone who opens doors that
otherwise would not have been possible to have been opened. So Napoleon Hill
used a Blackie story with Andrew Carnegie.
There is the “Eureka!” story of the sudden realization, the light bulb finally went
off. There is the “inspired by” story that you were motivated to become what it
Classic Story Plots Most Useful to
Info-marketers
Charles Atlas / Rocky / “They All
Laughed…”
The Little Engine Who Could – proves
you can too
Blackie
“Eureka!”
Inspired By…
Lost/Hidden Secrets
/ Amazing Discoveries
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 28 of 29 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 29
© 2014 GKIC Module 4
is that you are because of some persuasive story that makes a point about what
you are going to sell.
Of course there are the lost secrets, the hidden secrets, the amazing discovery
story, which again if you look at the financial stuff I showed you in module #1 to
show you the code, you will also find these stories used liberally by those
marketers.
So the little engine that could, who proves you can too; remember I showed you
the IRS worker who turns $1,842 into a million. Little guy doing big things.
The hidden secrets, I showed you George Bush’s secret retirement legacy.
You will find these stories almost everywhere that you look, being used by good
info-marketers in almost every category.
If you want to make more money, you will tell more stories,
and you will tell stories better.
The more adept you become at story-telling, the more you build up your bank of
good stories that can be tweaked and used for different purposes as you need
them and the better you are at telling them, the more money you will make.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 29 of 29 --
